items.headline: Somalia enters constitutional vacuum as parliament term expires without succession plan
items.body: Somalia's federal parliament term expired on April 14 with no electoral roadmap in place, reviving memories of the 2021 standoff when election disputes triggered armed clashes in Mogadishu. President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud has one month left in his mandate before it expires on May 15, while breakaway states Puntland and Jubbaland reject last-minute constitutional amendments extending legislative terms from four to five years.

items.headline: Zimbabwe declares state of disaster after commuter bus explosion kills 18 on major highway
items.body: A minibus carrying suspected explosive materials burst into flames on the Bulawayo-Beitbridge Road on April 16, killing at least 18 passengers between the Chipangali and Gwanda tollgate. President Mnangagwa declared a state of disaster following the blast on one of Zimbabwe's busiest routes linking the country to South Africa.

items.headline: Japan pledges 10 billion dollars to Southeast Asian countries hit by Middle East energy crisis
items.body: Japanese Prime Minister Takaichi Sanae announced funding equivalent to 1.2 billion barrels of crude oil to help Asian countries secure petroleum supplies after the closure of the Strait of Hormuz disrupted regional supply chains. The package matches the value of crude oil imported by all Southeast Asian nations in one year, Japan's Foreign Ministry stated.
